description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Redesign Den onboarding into a guided, more full-screen flow with clearer loading and browser-access states.
- Simplify OpenWork Share around single-skill publishing, with cleaner editor/frontmatter handling and a smoother shared-skill import path.
Fixes & polish
- Add a polished feedback entrypoint card in Settings.
- Slim down session sidebar density and add a ChatGPT provider tip to empty-state quickstarts.
- Remove legacy share-ready messaging, dead grain code, and stabilize share CTA and GitHub badge presentation.
Packaging / ops
- Update AUR packaging metadata for the release.
- Publish release version 0.11.148 across app, desktop, orchestrator, server, and router packages.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.147...v0.11.148
